texts.facebook:
You've finished 12 tutorials. Still no interview calls.

Here's the gap: watching someone else code isn't the same as solving a real business case under pressure.

ML Career Simulator hires you as a Junior ML Engineer at fictional company Datacore. You grow to Middle by working through 20 real business cases.

3 advanced modules are built directly on "Machine Learning System Design" by Babushkin & Kravchenko (Manning) — the same frameworks real ML system designers use.

Start free: https://ml-simulator-app-production.up.railway.app/en.html?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=organic&utm_content=2026-07-22-product-5db6a7
texts.linkedin:
Tutorials taught you the theory. They didn't teach you how to ship.

That's the gap between finishing a course and getting hired as an ML Engineer: recruiters don't ask if you watched a video series, they ask what you built and how you'd design a system under real constraints.

ML Career Simulator closes that gap differently. You're hired as a Junior ML Engineer at fictional company Datacore, and you progress to Middle level by working through 20 real business cases — the kind that show up in actual interviews and actual jobs.

Three of the advanced modules are built directly on "Machine Learning System Design" by Babushkin & Kravchenko (Manning), so you're learning the same frameworks used by practicing ML system designers, not a simplified version of them.

If you're tired of tutorials that don't translate into offers, this is worth 20 minutes of your time.

Start free: https://ml-simulator-app-production.up.railway.app/en.html?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=organic&utm_content=2026-07-22-product-5db6a7

#MachineLearning #MLEngineer #DataScience #CareerGrowth #TechJobs
